newsSince: RBC's fiscal momentum remains strong: backlog crossed $1B for the first time (mostly aerospace & defense), and management confirmed substantially increased content on the GTF Advantage engine ramping from CY2026 through 2030. All material catalysts are aerospace/defense-driven, not robotics or automated-manufacturing driven.

sources.label: RBC Q1 FY2026 earnings call highlights (Yahoo)

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent screen: backlog >$1B and GTF Advantage content gains are real but purely aerospace/defense, orthogonal to the robotics/automated-manufacturing repurposing thesis this page tests. No evidence the precision-bearing footprint is feeding the AI-node bottleneck. Prior hold stands.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Aerospace/defense backlog and GTF content ramp
verdicts.primaryRisk: Thesis is A&D-driven, not latent-AI; no robotics linkage
verdicts.bull: Record backlog, GTF Advantage content ramp, A&D aftermarket +22.6% give durable high-margin compounding.
verdicts.neutral: Strong cyclical exec, but no read-through to the AI-node/robotics latent pathway the screen prices.
verdicts.bear: Pure defense/aero cyclical; if AI-node thesis is the lens, RBC has zero incremental relevance.
